name: Builder.io Visual CMS and Headless API
what_it_does: Visual CMS and page builder with REST and GraphQL API — enabling marketing teams to visually build and edit pages within any React, Next.js, Vue, Nuxt, or Angular app without engineering involvement, connecting a drag-and-drop visual editor to the live application using real code components, with A/B testing, personalization, and scheduling built in.
use_cases: Delivering headless content from Builder.io via REST or GraphQL API for React/Next.js integration
Enabling marketing teams to visually build landing pages and sections within live apps via Builder SDK
Running A/B tests and managing content experiments via Builder.io API
Personalizing content variations via Builder.io Targeting API for visitor segment delivery
Scheduling content publishes and managing content lifecycle via Builder.io API
not_for: Pure content management without visual editing (use Contentful or Sanity for content-only headless)
Enterprise DXP with complex workflow and approval requirements
best_when: A marketing team needs to build and edit landing pages, sections, and content within an existing React/Next.js app without developer involvement — while developers register code components for marketers to compose visually.
avoid_when: You don't use React/Vue/Angular, need complex content workflows with approvals, primarily need content-only API without visual editing, or are building a content-heavy editorial site.

auth.notes: API key (public and private) from Builder.io organization. Public key for content delivery (safe in client-side). Private key for write operations (server-side only). Bearer token auth. Webhook for content change events. REST API + GraphQL for content delivery. Builder SDK for visual editor integration. React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Qwik SDK packages. Integration with Shopify, Gatsby, Next.js, Nuxt.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 2 spaces, basic features, limited API calls
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2018 by Steve Sewell (former Auth0 engineer). Raised $14M Series A (2021). Backed by Gradient Ventures (Google), CRV. Unique visual editor that edits live React/Vue apps (not separate preview). Marketing teams can edit without breaking code. 60,000+ websites using Builder.io. AI-powered design-to-code import (Figma to Builder components). Growing rapidly in JAMstack ecosystem. Storybook integration for developer component registration. Next.js integration is strongest use case.

performance.rate_limits: Based on plan tier; free tier has API call limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + GraphQL API. Public and private API key auth. Good developer documentation with Next.js integration guides. Webhook for content changes. SDK for React/Vue/Angular/Svelte integration. Public key safe for client-side delivery. Private key for mutations — server-side only. Free tier API call limits — verify before production. US data residency only.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Page reads idempotent by URL or model/content type. A/B test reads idempotent. Content creation uses content ID for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Public API key (safe for client) vs Private API key (mutations, server-side only) — critical distinction
US data residency only — EU customers need GDPR adequacy assessment
Free tier has API call limits — check limits before building production integration
React/Next.js SDK integration required for visual editing (REST API alone gives content only)
Visual editor requires registered React components — marketing teams can compose but not create new components

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. Public + private API key auth. Gradient Ventures (Google) backing implies security investment. Public key safe for client exposure (read-only delivery). Private key must be server-side — never expose in client-side code or public repos. Visual editor runs in iFrame — content isolation between editor and live app. A/B testing may expose unreleased content — configure targeting carefully.
